about.page.content.about.title: About
about.page.content.about.description: Explore Vidvix, its core capabilities, and the open-source projects it builds on.
about.page.content.about.version.label: Version
about.page.content.about.author.label: Author
about.page.content.about.contact.label: Contact author & bug reports
about.page.content.about.website.label: Official website
about.page.content.about.summary.label: Product overview
about.page.content.about.summary.title: A local-first offline media workspace
about.page.content.about.summary.descrip: Vidvix brings local media processing, AI-assisted workflows, and desktop utility features into one Windows interface so common tasks can stay as offline and direct as possible.
about.page.content.about.capabilities.de: The current experience is centered around these day-to-day media and desktop workflows:
about.page.content.about.capabilities.it: Video, audio, trim, merge, AI, split-audio, and terminal modules share output planning, progress feedback, and result reveal patterns.
The app hot-switches between Simplified Chinese and English, and supports system, light, and dark appearance preferences.
Bundled media and AI runtimes are maintained in-repo and preferred locally when available, reducing network dependency.
System tray behavior, window state persistence, shortcut creation, and copyable media details keep the workflow grounded in Windows desktop use.
about.page.content.about.openSource.desc: Some of Vidvix's core capabilities are made possible by these open-source projects. Click a project name to open its homepage or repository.
about.page.content.about.openSource.item: Handles media conversion, probing, track extraction, and the allowlisted command execution inside Terminal.
Powers preview and embedded playback surfaces for workflows that need visual feedback.
Drives offline four-stem source separation in the Split Audio workspace.
Provides the offline interpolation runtime behind the AI interpolation workflow.
Provides the offline super-resolution and enhancement runtime behind the AI enhancement workflow.
about.page.content.license.title: License
about.page.content.license.description: Review license information for Vidvix itself, third-party components, and bundled offline runtime assets.
about.page.content.license.summary.label: License overview
about.page.content.license.summary.title: First-party and third-party licenses
about.page.content.license.summary.descr: This page summarizes the license information that matters to the current Vidvix repository snapshot and offline distribution flow, with emphasis on the app itself, bundled media and AI toolchains, and notice files preserved inside offline runtime packages.
about.page.content.license.firstParty.de: The following item applies to the Vidvix project's own source code and project-authored materials.
about.page.content.license.thirdParty.de: The following components are the major third-party projects referenced or bundled by the current repository and offline release flow.
about.page.content.license.notes.title: Distribution and compliance notes
about.page.content.license.notes.descrip: Third-party components remain governed by their upstream terms; review the corresponding notice requirements before repackaging, replacing, or redistributing runtime assets.
about.page.content.license.item.vidvix.d: Applies to the original source code and project-authored materials in this repository. It does not replace or override the licenses of any third-party runtimes, models, or binaries.
about.page.content.license.item.vidvix.n: The repository root LICENSE applies to Vidvix itself; third-party components must still follow their own upstream licenses.
about.page.content.license.item.ffmpeg.l: The upstream FFmpeg project is LGPL 2.1+; GPL 2+ applies when GPL components are enabled
about.page.content.license.item.ffmpeg.d: Handles media conversion, media inspection, track extraction, and allowlisted command execution inside Terminal.
about.page.content.license.item.ffmpeg.n: The current repository snapshot preserves Tools/ffmpeg/LICENSE.txt (GNU LGPL v3 text). Final redistribution obligations should be validated against the exact FFmpeg build you ship.
about.page.content.license.item.mpv.lice: GPL v2+ by default; LGPL 2.1+ when built with non-GPL configuration
about.page.content.license.item.mpv.desc: Provides the playback core behind preview and embedded playback workflows that need visual feedback.
about.page.content.license.item.mpv.noti: The current repository snapshot includes Tools/mpv/mpv-1.dll. Release packages should retain the notice files that correspond to the exact libmpv build shipped.
about.page.content.license.item.demucs.v: demucs 4.0.1; runtime lock also includes Python 3.10.11, Torch 2.5.1, and Torchaudio 2.5.1
about.page.content.license.item.demucs.d: Drives offline four-stem source separation for the Split Audio workspace, with runtime preparation handled through bundled offline packages and models.
about.page.content.license.item.demucs.n: Tools/Demucs/Packages/demucs-runtime-win-x64-*.zip contains embedded third-party license and notice files for the packaged runtime environment.
about.page.content.license.item.rife.des: Provides the offline interpolation runtime used by the AI interpolation workflow, with the current repository retaining the `rife-v4.6` launch model and config.
about.page.content.license.item.rife.not: Bundled notice file: Tools/AI/Licenses/rife-ncnn-vulkan-LICENSE.txt. Source and retained assets are tracked in Tools/AI/Manifests/rife.json.
about.page.content.license.item.realesrg: Provides the offline super-resolution and enhancement runtime used by the AI enhancement workflow, including the executable and launch configuration kept in the current repository.
Bundled notice file: Tools/AI/Licenses/realesrgan-ncnn-vulkan-LICENSE.txt. Source and retained assets are tracked in Tools/AI/Manifests/realesrgan.json.
Covers the Standard and Anime model set currently retained for the AI enhancement workflow.
about.page.content.license.notes.item.bu: The current repository already preserves the root LICENSE, Tools/ffmpeg/LICENSE.txt, Tools/AI/Licenses/*, and multiple upstream license files inside the Demucs runtime package.
Build-specific obligations for FFmpeg and mpv
Final obligations for these components can vary with the exact build configuration. Official release packages should always keep the notice files that match the shipped binaries.
about.page.content.license.notes.item.ru: The Demucs offline runtime package contains Python, Torch, Torchaudio, and other dependencies. If you redistribute an extracted runtime, keep the accompanying license files with it.
about.page.content.privacy.title: Privacy
about.page.content.privacy.description: This area will be used for privacy notes and data usage details. More content will be added later.
